title: Pods, Multi-Container Composition, Lifecycle, Termination, and Debug Containers · Kubernetes Operations Room
summary: A platform pairing session predicts each API transition before producing the first working bounded Pod composition and lifecycle manifest for pods, multi-container composition, lifecycle, termination, and debug containers. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bounded Pod composition and lifecycle manifest while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain Pod identity, scheduling unit, shared network and volumes, isolated process and filesystem boundaries, and ephemeral replacement.
Choose init, app, native sidecar, adapter, ambassador, and helper containers from startup, restart, ownership, shutdown, and resource contracts.
Trace waiting, running, terminated, reasons, exit codes, restartPolicy, backoff, previous state, and Pod phase without equating them.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Pod Shared Boundaries
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Pod has an init container that never completes, a sidecar that outlives the app, an ephemeral debug container, restart count 6, and an emptyDir holding the only diagnosis. Explain phase, container state, restart, sharing, and replacement boundaries. Competency probe: Explain Pod identity, scheduling unit, shared network and volumes, isolated process and filesystem boundaries, and ephemeral replacement.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Pod Shared Boundaries.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain pod identity, scheduling unit, shared network and volumes, isolated process and filesystem boundaries, and ephemeral replacement. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Pod Shared Boundaries, keep K8s Pod Shared Boundaries connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A workshop Pod has an init container that never completes, a sidecar that outlives the app, an ephemeral debug container, restart count 6, and an emptyDir holding the only diagnosis. Explain phase, container state, restart, sharing, and replacement boundaries. Competency probe: Explain Pod identity, scheduling unit, shared network and volumes, isolated process and filesystem boundaries, and ephemeral replacement.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which K8s Pod Shared Boundaries condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Kubernetes Workloads, Configuration, Probes, and Autoscaling — Controls Pods, init and sidecar containers, Deployments, StatefulSets, DaemonSets, Jobs, CronJobs, configuration, lifecycle, probes, resources, QoS, disruption, and HPA.

At Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Pod Shared Boundaries, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A workshop Pod has an init container that never completes, a sidecar that outlives the app, an ephemeral debug container, restart count 6, and an emptyDir holding the only diagnosis. options.text: State the K8s Pod Shared Boundaries preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
A Pod is a durable virtual machine and its IP, local data, and identity survive replacement. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.

For commit to the mission model, copy one familiar manifest or kubectl command, omit context, API discovery, desired-versus-observed status, failure, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer checks, then accept an Applied response as production proof.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Ephemeral Debug Container Limits
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-ephemeral-debug-container-limits-p8dy69". Tailor its pod.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Ephemeral Debug Container Limits.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Ephemeral Debug Container Limits into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Ephemeral Debug Container Limits, keep K8s Ephemeral Debug Container Limits connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-ephemeral-debug-container-limits-p8dy69
  - competency: k8s-ephemeral-debug-container-limits
    artifact: pod.yaml
    decision: ephemeral debug container limits
    environment: managed canary cluster with provider-specific load balancing
    fault-injection: withdraw the selected image digest after one node pulls it
    retained-proof: object UID generation observedGeneration and status conditions
    case-key: p8dy69
    change: |
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: Pod
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      initContainers: [schema-check]
      containers: [api, telemetry-sidecar]
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
    verify: kubectl get pod status-api -o json && kubectl logs status-api --previous -c api
    failure: Reject durable-Pod assumptions, unbounded sidecars, swallowed signals, missing previous logs, or debug mutation presented as image repair.
    repair: Assign container ownership, bound startup and shutdown, capture states and exit causes, and prove a clean replacement preserves required behavior.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

Competency probe: Use ephemeral containers and shared process evidence for bounded debugging while preserving image immutability and naming remaining node-level limits.
